step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("distinguishing characteristics between herpes zoster and other vesico-bullous eruptions")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed review of the literature, there remains compelling support for option \( \textbf{A}: \) Unilateral occurrence. Multiple sources consistently emphasize the unique dermatomal, unilateral distribution of herpes zoster as a distinguishing feature compared to other vesiculobullous eruptions like impetigo, contact dermatitis, and even less obvious mimics such as Stevens-Johnson Syndrome/TEN, erythema multiforme, and hand-foot-and-mouth disease. While options B-C-D offer valid symptomatic descriptions, they do not capture the defining localization hallmark shared universally among cases of true herpes zoster. Therefore, my final stance firmly agrees with earlier conclusions emphasizing the unilateral dermatomal pattern as central to diagnosing herpes zoster accurately amidst competing vesiculobullous presentations.
Answer Choice: \(\boxed{\text{A}}\)
